text:
is the novel version of the movie Hunter × Hunter: Phantom Rouge and based on Yoshihiro Togashi's Hunter × Hunter. It was written by Hajime Tanaka and scripted by Shoji Yonemura. The novel was released by Shueisha on January 15 th , 2013.

The novel includes a canon flashback of Kurapika's childhood adapted from the special chapters Kurapika's Memories Part One and Two, which was a manuscript written by Yoshihiro Togashi that he eventually ended up scrapping from the main story. The plot of the novel itself is considered filler.

The novel version includes details from Kurapika's Memories that were left out of the movie. It also contains extra scenes, some of which were animated and released in a trailer, but were also ultimately left out of the movie.

Blurb

Kurapika's Scarlet Eyes were stolen!? Having narrowly escaped with his life, he shares that unbelievable truth with his friends. The one responsible was a young boy of the Kurta Clan, a clan in which Kurapika should have been the sole survivor. Behind it all lurks the shadow of the Phantom Troupe... This is the complete novelization of the renowned movie version!

Pre-Index Contents

Before the prologue, the novel includes a profile page with the character design illustrations and short descriptions for the following characters and groups.
Gon Freecss
Killua Zoldyck
Kurapika
Leorio Paradinight
Hisoka Morow
Illumi Zoldyck
Omokage
Pairo
Retz
Phantom Troupe

Contents

Prologue: Page 11
Chapter 1: Page 17
Chapter 2: Page 59
Chapter 3: Page 113
Chapter 4: Page 171
Epilogue: Page 237

Locations

Lusko Province
Shanha City
Totoria District

Plot

Prologue

The prologue describes a beautiful forest and the 128 people residing there. Kurapika was bound by the rule that one must not leave the forest. In actuality, no one attempted to break this rule. The forest was a sanctuary for the Kurta Clan and completely isolated from the outside world. For young Kurapika and his best friend, this was their entire world, and they had no reason to doubt their way of life in the forest. During his life there, Kurapika was told by his best friend that only the people of the Kurta Clan have eyes that can turn scarlet when their emotions are elevated.

For this reason, only "adults" who can keep secrets are permitted to leave, as the people of the outside world discriminate against those who are different from them. They grew curious about the outside world after receiving an adventure book from a lost traveler. The book contained experiences that Kurapika and his friend could never experience in the forest, so they were instantly captivated. Kurapika read the book repeatedly until it was worn and began to dream of the outside world and the noble profession of a Hunter.

Kurapika recalls the words of his best friend from the morning of his departure. He was always at Kurapika's side, supporting him. He had sacrificed his eyesight and legs to save Kurapika from falling off a cliff. He had accompanied Kurapika as his partner during the test for obtaining permission to leave the village. In the middle of the test, he tries to soothe Kurapika, who is furious with some thugs, and even gets on his hands and knees to apologize. Thanks to his tact, he outwitted the Elder, allowing Kurapika to pass a test he would have otherwise failed. Without him, Kurapika would have never been able to go to the outside world, nor would he still be alive. Kurapika promises his friend that he will find a doctor to treat his eyes and legs. 

To this day, Kurapika has never once forgotten about his friend. About his friend's smile and wave goodbye, and the day they promised to go on an adventure together. At the Kurta Clan massacre scene, a note was left among his brethren's brutally slaughtered bodies. His promise to his friend could never be fulfilled, leaving Kurapika stunned. He is face-to-face with a young boy, who is saddened by their current situation. Kurapika's eyes, now devoid of light, reflected only darkness. He is wounded and collapsed on the ground, unable to move. The boy tells Kurapika that he will put him out of his misery, but wants him to believe that he is still his best friend. However, Kurapika refuses to believe that Pairo, the boy standing before him with his sword raised, would ever attempt to take his life.

Chapter 1

The story begins shortly after the events of Yorknew. Gon and Killua are traveling by airship to a small rural town called Shanha, where Kurapika has been hospitalized. During the journey, Killua reflects on his friendship with Gon, feeling a deep sense of gratitude toward him, much like the way Kurapika once thought about his childhood friend, Pairo.

At the airport, Gon and Killua meet Leorio, who explains what happened to Kurapika. Not long after parting ways in Yorknew, Kurapika had heard rumors about a surviving member of the Kurta clan. Driven by hope and unable to ignore the possibility, he set aside his work and traveled to investigate the matter. Leorio, concerned about Kurapika’s emotional vulnerability when it came to his clan, accompanied him. Though he suspected from the beginning that the rumors were false, he stayed by Kurapika’s side to support him.

In Shanha, Kurapika was wandering the streets when he heard a familiar melody drifting from an alleyway. Following the sound, he discovered Pairo playing a stringed instrument. To Kurapika’s shock, Pairo looked the same as he had the day Kurapika last saw him—he hadn’t aged at all. Though Kurapika found this strange, the overwhelming joy of reunion drowned out his doubts, and he embraced his childhood friend. But when he looked closer, he realized that Pairo’s eyes were gone—only darkness remained.

Pairo then asked for Kurapika’s eyes. Before Kurapika could process what was happening, Pairo used a mysterious Nen ability to steal them. Leorio, who was nearby, witnessed the event but could not grasp its details, lacking the ability to use Gyo. Afterward, Pairo attacked both Leorio and Kurapika, wielding twin wooden swords in what appeared to be a traditional Kurta fighting style. Pairo claimed that he did not want to fight, yet his actions showed otherwise as he tried to kill Kurapika. The latter, devastated and unable to comprehend why his beloved friend would do such a thing, stood paralyzed in the rain, weeping. In that moment, Leorio lifted Kurapika into his arms and fled, carrying him to safety.

With no proper medical facilities in Shanha, Leorio treated Kurapika himself in the run-down local hospital. When Gon and Killua arrived to visit, they found Kurapika lying in darkness, robbed of his sight. Yet through the Nen ability that had stolen his eyes, he began experiencing visions. He saw a mysterious man marked with a spider tattoo of the number four. The man, standing in ancient ruins, addressed Kurapika with the words, “It is time to wake up, my angel.” Through the ruins’ window, Kurapika could glimpse the surrounding landscape, leading the group to realize that his stolen eyes were still linked to him.

Determined to use this to their advantage, the group analyzed the details of the landscape Kurapika described. Using the Hunter website, they compared the features and eventually pinpointed the region: Totoria, not far from their current location. With Kurapika still gravely injured and Leorio staying behind to care for him, Gon and Killua decided to proceed with the investigation. As they prepared to leave, Gon nearly forgot his Hunter License in the computer. Killua scolded him for his carelessness, but Gon instead entrusted the license to Killua, believing it would be safer with him. Killua accepted, and the two set off toward Totoria, eager to uncover the mystery and save Kurapika.

Chapter 2

Gon and Killua split up while searching for clues, and Gon encounters Retz, a young puppeteer. After saving Retz and a little girl from a carriage accident, Gon befriends him, though he feels an odd sense of unease when looking into his eyes. Retsu claims to know about the place they’re searching for, so Gon introduces him to Killua under the clock tower. Killua is immediately suspicious and reluctant to include Retsu, but Gon insists.

The three head to their hotel, where Killua warns Gon that Retsu might be a thief. Gon dismisses the concern, only for both boys to discover by accident that Retsu is actually a girl disguised as a boy. At dinner, she explains her reasons, demonstrates her puppetry skills, and compliments both Gon’s and Killua’s eyes, leaving the latter flustered. Though Killua wonders momentarily if she might be tied to the theft of Kurapika’s clan’s eyes, he dismisses the idea.

Meanwhile, Hisoka sneaks into Kurapika’s hospital room and ominously hints that he knows the identity of the one who stole Kurapika’s eyes. That same night, Gon, Killua, and Retsu are attacked at their hotel by Uvogin, who should be long dead. Killua quickly realizes they are outmatched and urges Gon to escape, but Gon refuses to abandon the fight even while badly injured. Just as Uvo is about to finish them, Nobunaga intervenes and reveals the truth: this Uvogin is a puppet created by Omokage.

Omokage’s ability allows him to create living puppets from others’ memories, complete with personalities, but bound to his will. He can control unlimited puppets, and to make them stronger, he uses parts of real corpses—like Uvogin’s eyes. As a self-proclaimed “collector”, Omokage expresses interest in Gon’s and Killua’s eyes. Before Nobunaga can retaliate for Uvo’s desecration, Omokage vanishes, leaving behind his puppets to stall the fight. Nobunaga and Machi handle them, warning Gon and Killua to stay out of Phantom Troupe affairs.

Back at the hospital, Hisoka reveals Omokage’s ability to Kurapika and Leorio. He explains his motives: if Kurapika dies, the Nen binding on Chrollo would grow stronger and harder to undo. For now, Hisoka wants Kurapika alive—at least until he gets his desired battle with Chrollo. He also mentions that he spared Omokage once only because he found the latter's ability fascinating.

 

Chapter 3

Gon takes Retz shopping for clothes, which only fuels Killua’s irritation as they linger too long. Killua drags Gon outside, insisting he still doesn’t trust Retz and warning that she might betray them. As he speaks, Illumi’s words echo in his mind about betraying friends. Unbeknownst to them, Retz eavesdrops and secretly uses her “Soul Doll” ability, sending a puppet into Killua’s shadow. She then reappears, claiming she understands his doubts but wants to help as a friend, guiding them to an eerie old mansion. At the entrance, Gon and Killua urge her to stay behind for her own safety, and though she agrees, Gon notices her eyes carry a deep sadness.

Inside the mansion, they encounter a puppet of Illumi. Even knowing it isn’t real, Killua freezes in fear. The puppet, eyeless, declares he’ll take Killua’s eyes, pinning Gon to the wall when he protests. Illumi repeats his familiar lines about betrayal, pointing out that Killua’s cautious retreat is already proof of it. Gon breaks free and attacks, but is thrown violently through a window, leaving him battered and bleeding. Illumi advances on Killua, but when Killua remains paralyzed, Gon leaps between them—only to have his own eyes stolen instead. Illumi vanishes to deliver the eyes to Omokage, leaving Killua devastated. Watching Retsu rush to Gon’s aid, Killua bitterly concludes that she is a better friend than he could ever be. Wracked with guilt, he begins to think Gon would have been safer if he had never been by his side at all.

Wandering aimlessly in the rain, Killua breaks down in despair. When a drunk man assaults him, Killua feels nothing but numbness and accepts Illumi’s words: killing is all he can do. He nearly strikes the man down when Gon suddenly appears—blind, yet guided by Killua’s scent and the sound of the scuffle. Gon embraces Killua tightly, stopping him. Overcome with shame, Killua insists he betrayed Gon and has returned to his old self. But Gon rejects this, saying the past doesn’t matter—what counts is the present. Even if Killua were to betray him, Gon says he wouldn’t mind. Killua, tearful, is moved by Gon’s unconditional trust.

Gon also reveals that he has suspected Retsu from the start. Her eyes didn’t match her words, and he realized she had been using Omokage’s power. When Gon allowed his own eyes to be stolen, it was intentional—so he could see where Omokage was hiding. Soon, Leorio and Kurapika arrive, tracking Gon’s phone. Kurapika has healed himself with his chains and, though still blind, can use Nen.

Meanwhile, Retz confronts Omokage. It is revealed that she is a puppet, created when Omokage experimented on her by transferring her eyes into a doll. Unable to return them, he remade her as a puppet and has since sought the perfect eyes for her, even letting her borrow his own. Though she mourns his loss of humanity, Omokage insists everything he does is for her sake.

When Gon and the others reach the mansion, they find it filled with grotesque dolls. Kurapika declares he must face Omokage himself, both as revenge for the Kurta massacre and for Omokage’s desecration of Pairo. Omokage appears with the Illumi puppet, Pairo—whose scarlet eyes belong to Kurapika—and Retsu at his side. Retsu apologizes to Gon for hurting him, but Gon assures her they’re still friends, even as he laments her life as a puppet bound by her brother’s will. Omokage, however, has lured them here intentionally—he wants Killua’s and Leorio’s eyes too. He unleashes his puppets: Gon and Killua are forced to battle the Illumi puppet, while Kurapika and Leorio face off against Pairo.

Chapter 4

Inside Omokage’s mansion, the group encounters more puppets—this time including Pairo. Though Pairo expresses happiness that Kurapika found new friends, he must obey Omokage’s orders: first to steal their eyes, and then to kill anyone who resists. Kurapika, still blind and hesitant to strike his former best friend, falters. But when Pairo attacks, Leorio throws himself in the way and urges Kurapika to let go of the past, reminding him that they must live in the present. Leorio’s words shake Kurapika free from his hesitation, and he destroys the Pairo puppet, though with sorrow and renewed hatred for Omokage’s cruelty.

Meanwhile, Killua struggles against the Illumi puppet, paralyzed by its endless repetition of Illumi’s old words. This time, however, Gon argues back against everything the puppet says, bolstering Killua’s courage. Together, they finally destroy the puppet and recover Gon's stolen eyes. But Omokage isn’t finished. He summons puppets of the Phantom Troupe members—a goal he had when he originally joined them. Exhausted from battle, Gon, Killua, Kurapika, and Leorio are overwhelmed. Hisoka appears, intrigued by the chance to test himself against the Troupe’s replicas, including Chrollo's puppet. While Hisoka holds them off, Kurapika goes after Omokage directly.

Omokage unveils his secondary ability, Doll Catcher, which allows him to absorb his puppets and wield their abilities. Having absorbed Franklin, he fires devastating Nen bullets, forcing the group to retreat outside. Their only hope is to seal him with Kurapika’s Chain Jail, but to do that, someone must distract him. Gon and Killua step forward, using their aura to deflect the bullets head-on. Amused, Omokage increases his firepower until both boys are drained. Killua shields Gon, but is riddled with bullets, and is thrown bleeding to the ground.

Seeing Killua near death ignites Gon’s fury, while Omokage took to Leorio as the next target. Leorio intercepts the blow, buying Kurapika an opening to bind Omokage—yet Omokage anticipates it and deflects the chain. With Gon exhausted, Leorio injured, and Killua collapsed, Kurapika stands alone. In desperation, Gon threatens to destroy his own eyes, declaring he doesn’t care if he sacrifices them if it means saving his friends. Omokage freezes—his obsession with eyes won’t let him risk losing them. At that same moment, Retz seizes a knife and destroys her own puppet eyes, breaking Omokage’s composure. Shaken, he falters long enough for Kurapika to bind him with Chain Jail, sealing his Nen successfully.

Leorio, barely conscious, administers emergency aid to Killua, who miraculously survives since Omokage avoided fatally damaging him in hopes of claiming his eyes intact. When Killua awakens, the group debates Omokage’s fate. Kurapika wishes to take revenge, but the others beg him not to stain himself with another killing. Before the decision is made, Retz steps forward and fatally stabs her brother, declaring she won’t allow him to hurt anyone again. Omokage accepts it, saying he’d rather die than live powerless without his ability.

Soon after, the real Phantom Troupe arrives, intent on erasing Omokage themselves. Finding him already dead, they prepare to confront Kurapika, but Shalnark calls them off—their mission is complete. They withdraw, and Hisoka disappears as well. In the aftermath, Retz sets the mansion ablaze, choosing to perish inside to prevent the creations from harming anyone else. Killua tries to rush in to save her, but Gon stops him, saying this is her decision and her peace to make. Leorio adds that people must follow their own goals and truths. Gon and Kurapika agree, though Killua admits he doesn’t have any goals of his own. Gon smiles and tells him that for now, it’s enough if Killua stays by his side.

Epilogue

After Gon and Kurapika recovered their eyes, and Killua and Leorio had their wounds treated, the four friends gathered at the airport to part ways. Gon and Killua set off once more in pursuit of Greed Island, while Leorio prepared to return to his studies, and Kurapika lingered in quiet thought.

He recalled his promise to Pairo—that when they met again, Pairo could ask him if he had enjoyed his journey into the outside world. For a moment, Kurapika wondered what his answer would be if the real Pairo stood before him. After all, his travels had been filled with pain, loss, and difficult battles.

Yet in the end, Kurapika found his answer to be an unwavering yes. Because alongside the sorrow, he had also found true companions. Even with the hardships, the friendships he gained made his journey worthwhile. And that alone was enough for him to keep his promise to Pairo.

Differences between Movie and Novel

The movie begins with Killua dreaming about Illumi, while the novel starts with Kurapika's past.
After Gon had his eyes stolen, Killua, ashamed and guilty, contemplated suicide as he stood in the path of a moving train. He was then saved and persuaded by Gon. In the novel, Killua was instead walking in the rain-covered alleyways when he was disturbed by a drunken man. Killua, almost caving in to his emotional turmoil, was about to kill the man when Gon stopped him. He hugged Killua tightly and confessed to his importance, letting Killua know that his past is irrelevant and he didn't care even if Killua betrayed him.
